children.heading: .—Federal government
children.label: 1 .—Federal government
children.text: The term “Federal Government” has the same meaning that the term “United States” had in the Act of (ch. 411, ) (known as the Davis-Bacon Act).March 3, 193146 Stat. 1494

children.heading: .—Wages, scale of wages, wage rates, minimum wages, and prevailing wages
children.label: 2 .—Wages, scale of wages, wage rates, minimum wages, and prevailing wages
A
children.text: the basic hourly rate of pay; and

children.label: i
children.text: the rate of contribution irrevocably made by a contractor or subcontractor to a trustee or to a third person under a fund, plan, or program; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the rate of costs to the contractor or subcontractor that may be reasonably anticipated in providing benefits to laborers and mechanics pursuant to an enforceable commitment to carry out a financially responsible plan or program which was communicated in writing to the laborers and mechanics affected.
